channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Retired Minnesota flag could receive ‘historic’ designation under new bill
item.description: Some state legislators say it’s important to commemorate the former flag. Others say problematic imagery and the old flag’s depiction of Indigenous Minnesotans is inappropriate.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Hibbing manufacturer responds to Trump's steel, aluminum tariffs
item.description: Canada is the biggest supplier of steel and aluminum for the U.S. The metals are used for products like nuts and bolts, sheet metal, machining and beer cans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Ireland cheats the US as its leader joins him to celebrate St. Patrick's Day
item.description: President Donald Trump welcomed Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin on Wednesday for the annual St. Patrick's Day celebration at the White House, where he added Ireland to the list of countries he says are taking advantage of the United States.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kuwait frees a group of jailed Americans, including contractors held on drug charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kuwait has released a group of American prisoners, including veterans and military contractors jailed for years on drug-related charges, in a move seen as a gesture of goodwill between two allies, a representative for the detainees told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit claiming tribal casinos in Minnesota are running illegal games
item.description: A federal district court judge has dismissed a lawsuit brought by one of Minnesota’s horse racing tracks against executives who oversee five tribal casinos in the state.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: The U.S. agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A Republican-backed bill would upend voter registration. Here are 8 things to know
item.description: Congressional Republicans are pushing legislation that would make sweeping changes to voter registration, including requiring those signing up to present documents proving U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: House GOP tees up final vote on bill to avert shutdown
item.description: House Republicans on Tuesday advanced their bill to avert an end-of-the-week government shutdown, teeing up a final vote on the legislation later in the day. The chamber voted 216-214 to adopt a rule — which governs debate on legislation — for Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) continuing resolution.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
